rulings.Case Title: SAMMIE BONNER VS. GEORGIA PACIFIC CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff¿s request for a continuance is denied. Plaintiff failed to submit an affidavit demonstrating that facts essential to justify opposition may exist and also demonstrating why such alleged facts cannot now be presented as required by C.C.P. 437c(h). Based on the denial of the request for a continuance, the motion for summary judgment is unopposed. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 18, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant BNSF Railway Company¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)
